# Draft Sequence

This anonymized sequence preserves the shape of the original drafting history without real names, organizations, titles, or local paths.

| Version | Shape | Trigger for next draft |
|---|---:|---|
| Long paper v1 | 30 pages | Author feedback: too long, too abstract, and full of generic framework references that did not help the intended reader. |
| Long paper v1.1 | 31 pages | Incremental edits did not fix the core problem. The paper still missed the executive audience and remained too long. |
| Directional outline v0.1 | 5 pages | First real restart. The work became shorter and more concrete, but still needed sharper structure. |
| Directional outline v0.2 | 5 pages | Parallel reviewer structure exposed missing moves: clearer ownership, clearer measures, and a concrete rollout frame. |
| Directional outline v0.3 | 5-6 pages | Reframed around one discipline instead of blending lifecycle, architecture governance, and blueprint reuse. |
| Directional outline v0.4 | 5-6 pages | Added facilitator role and four named lifecycle decisions after author feedback. |
| Directional outline v0.5 | 5-6 pages | Added cost-of-inaction framing that earlier versions had not made visible enough. |

Lesson:

The short directional spine should have been the first artifact. The long-paper attempts created avoidable iteration because frame, audience, and spine were not validated early.
